rows.headline: Karnataka SSLC Class 10 Results 2026 Declared at 12 PM Today
rows.summary: Karnataka's SSLC exam results for 2026 were officially released at noon on April 23 by Education Minister Madhu Bangarappa; 8,65,988 students who appeared at 2,870 centres statewide can now check their mark sheets on the KSEAB official websites.
rows.content:
Bengaluru, April 23 — The moment millions of students and parents across Karnataka had been eagerly waiting for has arrived. The SSLC (Class 10) examination results for 2026 were declared today at 12 PM, with Education Minister Madhu Bangarappa holding a press conference to officially release the results. Students can view their mark sheets on the official websites after the announcement.

## 8.65 Lakh Students' Results Announced

This year, examinations were conducted at a total of 2,870 centres across the state. A total of 8,65,988 students appeared for the exam, marking the first major milestone in their academic journey. Results are now accessible on the KSEAB official websites.

## Hindi Marks Instead of Grades

The government had previously considered awarding grades instead of numerical marks for the third language Hindi. However, following a court order, the Education Department decided to award marks as usual. Minister Madhu Bangarappa had met Chief Minister Siddaramaiah to discuss the pros and cons of this decision.

## CET Examinations Begin Today

Alongside the SSLC results, the Karnataka Common Entrance Test (CET) also commenced across the state today. A record 3,30,000 students have registered for the exam this year. Bengaluru has the highest concentration of candidates — 77,604 students appearing at 163 exam centres.

## Strict Security Measures for CET

To prevent malpractice, jammers have been installed at all CET examination centres. Police Commissioner Seemant Kumar Singh ordered prohibitory orders within a 200-metre radius around all exam centres. A special control room has been established at the Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) office to monitor the conduct of the examination.

rows.headline: Bengaluru Woman Pre-Ordered Handcuffs Online Before Burning Boyfriend Alive in Byadarahalli
rows.summary: Prema, who has been arrested, lured her boyfriend Kiran to her home with a promise of a 'western-style proposal,' then tied him up and set him on fire; police now reveal she purchased handcuffs and belts online just two days before the murder after Kiran began avoiding her and resumed contact with an ex-girlfriend.
rows.content:
**Bengaluru (April 22):** A murder case in Byadarahalli has taken explosive new turns as investigators uncover the premeditated nature of the crime. A woman named Prema lured her boyfriend Kiran to her home, telling him she would propose to him in a "western style." Once he arrived, she tied his hands and feet, stripped him of his clothes, poured kerosene over him, and set him on fire. Prema has since been arrested and is under police interrogation.

## Background: A Two-Year Relationship That Turned Bitter

Kiran and Prema had been acquainted for four years, with their romantic relationship lasting two years. However, Kiran recently began distancing himself from Prema and was reportedly back in contact with a former girlfriend. He even celebrated his ex-lover's birthday and took photographs with her.

When Prema found out, she confronted Kiran, sparking a major quarrel. Kiran's response was to block Prema's phone number entirely.

## The Lure and the Plan

Prema called Kiran from a different number, telling him she was willing to get married on the very date he had once promised. According to investigators, by this point she had already begun planning the murder.

Police have now confirmed that Prema pre-ordered handcuffs and a belt online just **two days before** the killing — pointing to a calculated, premeditated plan.

## Police Investigation

Detectives are now pursuing two key angles: whether Prema planned the murder entirely on her own — including researching methods online — or whether she had help from an accomplice.

## Family's Grief

Kiran was his parents' only son. His father and mother are inconsolable, and have tearfully demanded that Prema receive the harshest possible punishment for the crime.

rows.headline: Karnataka Heatwave Continues; Thunderstorms Forecast for Bengaluru This Evening
rows.summary: The IMD has warned that the heatwave will persist across Karnataka for another week, with Bengaluru recording 35–37°C and north Karnataka districts exceeding 40–41°C; thunderstorms and moderate rain are forecast for Bengaluru and several south interior districts this evening.
rows.content:
Bengaluru, Apr 23: Weather conditions continue to fluctuate across Karnataka, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasting thunderstorms in several south interior districts. A relatively cooler atmosphere has settled over Bengaluru, Mysuru, Mandya, and Chamarajanagar.

Bengaluru Urban, Bengaluru Rural, Mysuru, Hassan, Kodagu, and Kolar districts are likely to receive moderate rainfall during evening or night hours. Thunderstorms and strong winds have been forecast in some locations.

Despite cloudy conditions in South Canara, Udupi, and North Canara districts, temperatures are expected to remain high. High coastal humidity will make it feel even hotter.

Some parts of Belagavi, Dharwad, and Haveri districts may receive light drizzle. However, temperatures in Kalaburagi, Bidar, and Raichur districts are expected to exceed 40°C.

## Bengaluru

Maximum: 34–35°C | Minimum: 22°C. Overcast skies expected in the evening with a possibility of light rain.

## Mysuru and Kolar

Cloudy conditions expected, with the possibility of thunderstorms during evening or night. Maximum temperatures forecast around 33–35°C.

## Malnad Region

Scattered rainfall is expected, benefiting coffee plantations. Cool breezes and pleasant weather are forecast for the area.

## Hubballi-Dharwad

High temperatures expected, around 37–39°C. Conditions may turn drizzly or overcast by evening, with the possibility of dusty winds.

## Heatwave Warning

The IMD has warned that the heatwave will continue across the state for another week. Raichur and Kalaburagi have already recorded temperatures exceeding 41°C, while Bengaluru is currently at 36–37°C. Most districts are expected to see 40–42°C over the coming week.

## City-wise Temperature Forecast (Max °C / Min °C)

| Bengaluru | 35 | 22 |
| Mangaluru | 33 | 27 |
| Shivamogga | 37 | 23 |

| Mandya | 37 | 23 |
| Madikeri | 34 | 20 |

| Hassan | 34 | 21 |
| Chamarajanagar | 36 | 22 |

| Tumakuru | 36 | 23 |
| Udupi | 33 | 27 |
| Karwar | 31 | 27 |
| Chikkamagaluru | 33 | 21 |

| Haveri | 37 | 23 |

rows.headline: Kannada Actress Ranya Rao Released from Bengaluru Jail After One Year in Gold Smuggling Case
rows.summary: Kannada actress Ranya Rao, who was held at Parappana Agrahara prison for one year after being detained under COFEPOSA for allegedly smuggling gold worth over ₹102 crore, was granted bail by the Court of Economic Offences and released on April 22.
rows.content:
**Bengaluru, April 22:** Kannada actress Ranya Rao, arrested in a gold smuggling case, has been granted bail. The Court of Economic Offences approved the bail application and issued its order on Wednesday. After spending a year in prison, Ranya Rao walked free from Parappana Agrahara jail on Tuesday evening.

Ranya Rao had been lodged at Parappana Agrahara for the past year on charges of smuggling gold. A court had earlier granted her bail, but she remained in custody because a COFEPOSA (Conservation of Foreign Exchange and Prevention of Smuggling Activities Act) detention order was in force. With one year having elapsed since the COFEPOSA detention began, she was eligible for release. The Supreme Court had recently upheld the detention order issued by the Central Economic Intelligence Bureau (CEIB) against her under the Foreign Exchange Regulation Act and the Prevention of Smuggling Activities Act.

## Release After One Year

On March 3, 2025, actress Ranya Rao was arrested by the Directorate of Revenue Intelligence (DRI) on charges of smuggling gold worth more than ₹102 crore. She was subsequently sent to Parappana Agrahara prison. A single-judge bench of the High Court had dismissed her bail application. The CEIB issued a detention order under Section 3(1) of the COFEPOSA Act, which meant she could not obtain bail for the duration of one year.

## Background: Who Is Ranya Rao?

Ranya Rao had taken a break from films and was running several businesses. A resident of Dollars Colony, Bengaluru, she is the stepdaughter of senior IPS officer Ramachandra Rao, who is currently suspended in connection with the Rasaleele video scandal.

Customs officials had grown suspicious of Ranya Rao's frequent trips to Dubai under the guise of business. They kept a continuous watch on her activities. On March 3, 2025, when she arrived at Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru from Dubai, DRI officials conducted a search and found 14.213 kg of gold on her person. She was arrested on the spot. In the days that followed, further scandals involving her came to light one after another.

rows.headline: Bengaluru Gym Trainer's Wife Elopes with His Best Friend; Refuses to Return Home
rows.summary: Gym trainer Nitesh's wife, who had married him in a love marriage, eloped with his close friend Naveen after secretly carrying on a relationship behind his back. When Jayanagar police tracked them down, the woman refused to return to her husband and insisted on moving into PG accommodation instead.
rows.content:
Bengaluru, Apr. 22: A series of cases involving wives leaving their husbands for other men has been making headlines across Karnataka. The latest incident has emerged from Bengaluru, where a gym trainer's wife eloped with his best friend in a brazen betrayal of trust.

## Romance That Began in a Friend's Home

Gym trainer Nitesh and a young woman fell in love and, after persuading both families, got married. Nitesh and his friend Naveen had met while working together at a private company and had grown close. After the wedding, the couple moved into a rented house owned by Naveen's family.

During this period, Nitesh noticed that Naveen had developed an inappropriate interest in his wife. The wife was secretly calling Naveen while hiding it from her husband, and had even saved Naveen's number on her phone under a girl's name to conceal the contact. Nitesh confronted her about this multiple times and advised her to stop.

## Move to Banashankari Changes Nothing

Fed up with the situation, Nitesh eventually moved the family to a different rented house in Banashankari. But the old habits continued — the wife kept up her contact with Naveen. When Nitesh warned her again, she brushed off his concerns: "He is like a brother to me," she told him. She has now eloped with that very same person.

## The Escape

Three days ago, the wife quietly packed her bags, claimed a stomach ache, took a half-day leave from work, and slipped away with Naveen.

## Police Complaint and Shocking Statement

A distraught Nitesh filed a missing person complaint at Jayanagar Police Station, and a case was registered. Officers tracked down both the woman and Naveen and brought them to the station. There, the wife made a stunning declaration before police:

"Under no circumstances will I go back to my husband — I will go to PG accommodation instead."

A broken Nitesh pleaded before the police: "I have been betrayed — please give me justice."

Jayanagar police are continuing their investigation into the matter.

rows.headline: NIA Court Sentences Lashkar-e-Taiba Member T. Naseer and Six Others to 7 Years for Terror Plot Hatched Inside Bengaluru Prison
rows.summary: A special NIA court has sentenced seven accused, including Lashkar-e-Taiba operative T. Naseer, to seven years rigorous imprisonment for conspiring to radicalise inmates and plan terror attacks across India from inside Bengaluru's Parappana Agrahara Central Prison.
rows.content:
**Bengaluru, April 22:** A special National Investigation Agency (NIA) court has sentenced seven accused — including T. Naseer, a key accused and member of Lashkar-e-Taiba — to seven years of rigorous imprisonment for hatching a terror conspiracy from inside a prison to carry out attacks across the country, officials said.

The convicted individuals are Naseer, Sayed Suhail Khan, Mohammad Umar, Zahid Tabrez, Sayed Muddassir Pasha, Mohammad Faisal Rabbani, and Salman Khan. In addition to the jail term, a fine of ₹48,000 has been imposed on each.

The NIA stated that a Lashkar-e-Taiba-linked terror plot was orchestrated under Naseer's leadership inside the Bengaluru Parappana Agrahara Central Prison. All the accused had already pleaded guilty in the case. According to investigators, the accused had planned to identify, recruit, train, convert, and radicalise young inmates with the intention of carrying out terror strikes across India. The Bengaluru Central Crime Branch (CCB) had first registered the case in July 2023 after seizing weapons, ammunition, and digital devices.

The NIA took over the investigation after it emerged that the accused had also planned terror attacks within Bengaluru itself. Notably, T. Naseer was already serving a life sentence in connection with the 2008 Bengaluru serial bomb blasts case. Among those arrested, Salman Khan was extradited to India from the Republic of Rwanda. The NIA had filed chargesheets against 11 arrested accused and one absconding accused, Junaid Ahmad.

rows.headline: Bitcoin Scam Kingpin 'Sriki' Lived High on Others' Money: ED Uncovers Crores in Benami Assets
rows.summary: Following ED raids on his Jayanagar residence, it has emerged that Bengaluru Bitcoin fraud kingpin Srikrishna ('Sriki') accumulated crores worth of property in benami names while deliberately avoiding personal bank accounts, phones, or devices — funding a lavish lifestyle of chartered flights, luxury stays, and luxury cars on stolen funds.
rows.content:
Bengaluru, April 22: Bitcoin King Sriki's lifestyle rivalled that of the wealthiest. Despite owning nothing in his own name, he lived extravagantly on other people's money. In the wake of ED raids, his activities are being exposed one by one — Sriki had accumulated crores worth of property registered in benami (third-party) names.

## Knowing Sriki Proved Costly for Nalpad

Srikrishna alias Sriki was the kingpin of the Bitcoin scam. Yesterday, ED officials raided his residence and seized several documents. Sriki was living a lavish lifestyle in the house his father built in Jayanagar. His association with Sriki has now brought serious trouble for Nalpad. Mohammed Nalpad came to know Sriki through his brother Umar. Mohammed Nalpad and his brother Umar had Sriki as a mutual friend. Because of this connection — Sriki was Umar's classmate — Mohammed Nalpad and Haqib also fell under the ED's radar.

Sriki was a challenge for investigating agencies as well. He did not use his own mobile phone, did not own a personal laptop, and lived freely without a personal bank account or credit card. Dealing in crores worth of Bitcoin, Sriki was in extremely high demand. He was a drug addict who travelled by chartered flights and stayed in high-end flats and resorts. His associates arranged luxury cars for his transport.

## Competition to Keep Sriki in the Circle

Crime networks used hacker Srikrishna to hack government websites and poker gaming platforms, stealing crores worth of Bitcoin. There was fierce competition among criminal groups to keep Sriki close, as his presence facilitated hacking operations — from Bitcoin transfers to breaking into corporate databases. Police cases and arrests had become routine for hacker Sriki.

Avoiding all financial transactions through his own accounts and using no personal devices, Sriki had met international hackers while studying in Europe. Through these connections, he built hacker networks, and Srikrishna alias Sriki shared several details about his activities during police interrogation.

## Crores in Benami Properties

Sriki holds crores worth of assets registered in benami names. He owns property worth crores on Kanakapura Road at Kolagondanahalli, registered under two benami individuals — Sridhar and Manohar.

Overall, a large number of people exploited hacker Sriki to pocket crores. Meanwhile, Sriki himself — who hacked into others' accounts — led a high-flying lifestyle and has been a major headache for investigating agencies.

rows.headline: Fly91 Flight from Bengaluru to Hubballi Circles Sky for Over 2 Hours Due to Landing Problem — Second Incident in 5 Days
rows.summary: A Fly91 flight (IC3412) from Bengaluru to Hubballi was unable to land on arrival and circled in the air for more than two hours on Tuesday evening before finally touching down at 9:15 PM, marking the second such landing incident on the Hubballi route within five days and leaving passengers in panic.
rows.content:
**Hubballi:** A Fly91 flight from Bengaluru to Hubballi once again encountered a landing problem, forcing the aircraft to circle in the sky for more than two hours. This is the second such incident within five days.

Just days earlier, a Fly91 flight from Hyderabad was unable to land at Hubballi airport due to adverse weather conditions and had remained airborne for more than three hours. Now a similar problem has struck a flight arriving from Bengaluru.

Flight IC3412 departed Bengaluru on Tuesday evening at 6:00 PM and was scheduled to land at Hubballi airport by 6:50 PM. Although the aircraft reached Hubballi on time, it encountered a problem during the landing approach. As a result, the passenger-laden flight circled in the sky for over two hours before finally landing at 9:15 PM.

## Why Does This Keep Happening to Fly91?

In the wake of this incident, questions have been raised about why Fly91 flights alone are facing problems that no other airline's aircraft seem to encounter at Hubballi. When the fault occurred on the Hyderabad-origin flight, adverse weather was cited as the explanation. However, during the same window yesterday when the Fly91 flight was supposed to land, several other flights took off and landed without incident. The fact that only Fly91's aircraft faced a landing issue has raised suspicion and triggered outrage among passengers.

## Passengers Clutched Their Seats in Terror

As the Fly91 flight circled overhead for more than two hours with nowhere to land, passengers on board were gripped by intense fear. Many prayed to God, fearing for their lives, and sat frozen in their seats, bracing for the worst.
